nodes.label: 3D Tiles Materialized View Contract
nodes.rationale: pg2b3dm needs a flat geometry/properties view, so export is mediated by a stable materialized view contract.

nodes.label: Vertical Datum and EPSG:32650
nodes.rationale: Terrain z values are treated as elevations in the projected horizontal CRS context, but the vertical datum must be tracked explicitly for UAV clearance.

nodes.label: GGER / GeoSOT Encoding
nodes.rationale: GGER is treated as the external text form of GeoSOT/iBEST-DB gridcell, replacing BGC for display and API contracts.

nodes.label: 2D/3D Morton Digit Interleaving
nodes.source_location: §二维 GGER 编码 / §三维 GGER 编码

nodes.label: GeomGrids Obstacle Index
nodes.rationale: Derived geomgrids materialized views avoid redundant CityDB properties while enabling indexed grid-space computation.

nodes.label: PostgREST GGER + Box RPC
nodes.source_location: §13 实施状态：PostgREST GGER + Box RPC

nodes.label: GGER Airspace Visualization
nodes.rationale: The former Beidou/BGC visualization plan is replaced by GGER/GeoSOT level-aware 2D and vertical airspace rendering.

nodes.label: Camera-driven GGER Level Selection
nodes.source_location: §Zoom Level 到 GGER Level 策略

nodes.label: GGER Height Layer Stacking
nodes.source_location: §高度层生成方案 / §三维空域分割展示方案

nodes.label: Cesium Rendering and Performance Controls
nodes.source_location: §Cesium 渲染实现建议 / §性能控制

nodes.label: Check required iBEST-DB/PostGIS support and mandatory CityDB tables.
nodes.norm_label: check required ibest-db/postgis support and mandatory citydb tables.

nodes.label: Unified Flight Obstacle Field Contract
nodes.rationale: All obstacle source views expose identical fields so they can be UNIONed and refreshed independently without changing 3DCityDB standard tables.

nodes.label: Precision without schema breakage rationale
nodes.rationale: The plan raises XY/Z obstacle precision while preserving 3DCityDB, flight_obstacles, public wrapper, and GGER-only contracts.

nodes.label: Planning and Display Layer Separation
nodes.rationale: Path planning keeps fine terrain obstacles while the UI uses lower-cost LOD views, preventing visual optimization from reducing safety.
